cmdi.description: eval with variable — command injection if variable contains user input
eval with command substitution — double-evaluation injection
Command substitution containing variable — verify variable is sanitised
exec with variable — command injection if variable is user-controlled
bash -c with variable in string — injection if variable is user-controlled
sh -c with variable — injection if variable is user-controlled
xargs with variable argument — verify variable is not user-controlled
sudo with variable argument — privilege escalation if variable is user-controlled

unquoted_expansion.description: Unquoted variable in for loop — word splitting and glob expansion. Quote: "$var"
Unquoted variable in [ ] test — word splitting. Use [[ ]] or quote the variable
Unquoted variable as file path argument — glob expansion and word splitting. Quote: "$var"
echo with unquoted variable — glob expansion. Use echo "$var"

path_injection.description: PATH prepend without quoting — verify prepended directory is trusted
Current directory in PATH — allows hijacking commands via local files
PATH set from variable — verify variable is not user-controlled
LD_LIBRARY_PATH/LD_PRELOAD set — can redirect dynamic library loading

remote_code.description: curl | bash pattern — downloads and executes arbitrary code. Verify URL is trusted
wget | bash pattern — downloads and executes arbitrary code. Verify URL is trusted
Download to /tmp then execute — potential RCE
source <(curl ...) — remote code execution via process substitution

info_disclosure.description: Shell debug mode (set -x/-v) — prints commands with expanded values including secrets to stderr
Echoing sensitive variable — may appear in logs or terminal history
Printing all environment variables — may expose secrets

temp_file.description: Temp file with PID/RANDOM — predictable name allows symlink attacks. Use mktemp
mktemp -u (dry-run) — returns unused name without creating file, race condition. Omit -u
Fixed /tmp filename — predictable temp file can be hijacked. Use mktemp

secrets.description: Hardcoded PASSWORD in shell script — use secrets manager or env variable
